input.prompt:
My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?

data.content:
My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?
data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-02T02:38:36.521Z</current_datetime>

My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?

input.initialPrompt:
My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?

data.reasoningText: The user is asking about migrating from MSTest v3 to MSTest v4, specifically about the `[ExpectedException]` attribute which has been removed. Let me look at their test file to understand their current code and provide the right guidance.

data.content: MSTest v4 removed `[ExpectedException]`. The replacement is `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>()` (or `Assert.ThrowsException<T>()`). Let me update your code:
data.reasoningText: MSTest v4 removed `[ExpectedException]`. The replacement is `Assert.ThrowsException<T>()` (or `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>()` in newer versions). Let me update the test file to use `Assert.ThrowsException<T>()` and update the package version to MSTest v4.
